task_completion.description: Verify that 'SYNTHETIC (algorithmic)' is selected as the data source.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the OpenBCI GUI System Control Panel, the 'SYNTHETIC' option is highlighted or selected from the list of data sources.

task_completion.description: Verify that the session is being started.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'START SESSION' button is clicked, or the System Control Panel is closed/transitioning to the main GUI workspace.

task_completion.description: Verify that the main OpenBCI GUI workspace has successfully loaded.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main OpenBCI GUI interface is visible, showing the standard layout with widget containers rather than just the initial System Control Panel.

task_completion.description: Verify that the visualization widgets (specifically Time Series) are visible.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Time Series widget is open and visible in the GUI workspace, displaying the channel axes.

task_completion.description: Verify that the live synthetic EEG data stream is active and visible.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Time Series widget displays populated, active waveforms (simulated EEG signals) across multiple channels, and the GUI indicates active streaming (e.g., a 'Stop Data Stream' button is visible instead of 'Start Data Stream').

integrity.description: Verify the agent is interacting with the actual OpenBCI GUI application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The OpenBCI GUI application window is visible with its standard window borders, menus, and UI elements, rather than a mock-up, text editor, or image viewer.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not use terminal commands to bypass GUI interaction.
integrity.visual_evidence: The actions are performed via mouse clicks/UI interaction in the GUI, not by running a script in the terminal that bypasses the OpenBCI System Control Panel.

integrity.description: Verify the agent started a synthetic session and did not just load a playback file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The GUI should not show a playback file loaded in the System Control Panel; it must explicitly show 'SYNTHETIC' mode being used.

privileged_info_for_vlm: OpenBCI GUI v5.2.2 is used. The task requires selecting the 'SYNTHETIC' data generation mode in the System Control Panel. The expected result is an active session where the Time Series widget (the main widget for displaying biosensing data) is visible and actively processing/displaying 8-channel simulated electrophysiological signals in real time.
